sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשָׁכַּל to be bereaved of children, to be childless (Arab. ثَكِلَ, Aram. תְּכַל, ܬܒܠܰ), followed by an acc. Gen 27:45, לָמָּה אֶשְׁכַּלּ גַּם שְׁנֵיהֶם “why should I be bereaved of both them?” Gen 43:14, וַאֲנִי כַּאֲשֶׁר שָׁכֹלְתִּי שָׁכָֽלְתִּי “and I, if I be bereaved, I shall be bereaved,” the expression of a person who calmly bears what appears inevitable (compare Est 4:16). 1Sa 15:33. Part. pass. שְׁכוּלָה bereaved of children, Isa 49:21.

Piel שִׁכֵּל-

(1) to bereave, Gen 42:36 1Sa 15:33 (a) used of wild beasts devouring children, Lev 26:22, “I will send among you wild beasts that shall bereave you.” Eze 5:17 14:15 compare Hos 9:12 (b) of a sword as consuming the youths, Deu 32:25, מִחוּץ תְּשַׁכֶּל־חֶרֶב “without (in battle) the sword bereaves” (i.e. consumes the youths), Lam 1:20 Jer 15:7 -Eze. 36:1315 .

(2) to cause abortion (in women, flocks, etc.), used of an unhealthy soil, 2Ki 2:19, intrans. to make abortion, i.e. to suffer it (Plin. Ep. 8, 10), as a woman, Exo 23:26 a sheep or she-goat, Gen 31:38 hence to be sterile, as a vine, Mal 3:11. Part. מְשַׁכֶּלֶת subst. abortion, 2Ki 2:21.

Hiphil-

(1) like Piel, No. 1, b, to kill youths, Jer 50:9 (where מַשְׁכִּיל should be read, not מַשְׁכִּיל).

(2) to miscarry, Hos 9:14. See Piel No. 2.
